name: Jason Yates
subject: LEGISLATIVE UPDATE: Senate Blocks Born-Alive Bill
body:
| | Peter, Today, by a vote of 53 to 44, the Senate has blocked the Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act. The bill required 60 votes to overcome the legislative filibuster. This legislation was about one thing, a baby that survived an abortion receiving medical care. Yet, those opposed to the legislation argued in the same, tired way that this would limit women’s access to safe abortion and health care. Three Democrats – Senator Robert Case Jr (PA), Joe Manchin (WV), and Doug Jones (AL) - voted with Republicans in favor of the legislation. Every Democratic senator running for president voted against the bill. Three Republicans did not vote on the bill - Lisa Murkowski (AK), Kevin Cramer (ND), and Tim Scott (SC). We are extremely disappointed in these results and what it means for the view of life in our nation. Many of you sent a message to your Senators in support of this bill, and we thank you for taking action. We must continue to do so when there are so many aggressive voices choosing to value the convenience of choice over the sanctity of life. **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO

subject: A new day is coming for Venezuela
body:
Today, Vice President Mike Pence traveled to Bogota, Colombia, reaffirming President Donald J. Trump’s support for the people of Venezuela. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8902d139ea&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • February 25, 2019
---
| | |
# **A new day is coming for Venezuela**

**Today,****Vice President Mike Pence** traveled to Bogota, Colombia, reaffirming President Donald J. Trump’s support for the people of Venezuela in their struggle for freedom against the socialist dictatorship of Nicolas Maduro.

**Joined by interim President Juan Guaido** , whom President Trump officially recognized as Venezuela’s head of state last month, the Vice President stood near other leaders from the region “at a momentous hour for the people of Venezuela and for the progress of freedom in this hemisphere.”

**“Just days ago** , as the world watched, the tyrant in Caracas danced as his henchmen burned truckloads of food and medicine, and murdered civilians,” Vice President Pence said. “Saturday was a tragic day for the families of those who lost their lives . . . It was also a tragic day for the suffering people of Venezuela.”

**Quoting President Trump** , he looked to the future: “A new day is coming in Latin America.”

**The United States has imposed** tough sanctions on Maduro and members of his previous regime, blocking assets in the U.S. owned or controlled by Venezuela’s state-owned oil company. These actions build on international efforts to disconnect Maduro and his cronies from revenue sources, while preserving critical assets for the future of Venezuela.

**At the same time** , America continues to provide humanitarian assistance to the Venezuelan people. Food and medicine remain exempt from our sanctions. More than 3 million people have fled Venezuela since 2015—and that number may swell to more than 5 million by the end of 2019, according to the United Nations.

**The past few days** of suffering has only steeled America’s resolve to support the people of Venezuela, Vice President Pence said. He called the struggle at hand one between dictatorship and democracy, oppression and freedom, and pain versus prosperity.

**“To the good people** of Venezuela: Seek your freedom,” the Vice President said. “We will go with you. You go with God. _Vayan con Dios.”_

[Worth your time: Read Vice President Pence’s words to the people of Venezuela.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c8863cdbfa&e=3ee4985eea)

**A powerful photo:**[Vice President Pence to Juan Guaido: “We are with you 100%.”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=02369cf4c9&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **Our border crisis is real**

**Over the weekend,** Acting Defense Secretary Patrick Shanahan and Marine Gen. Joe Dunford—chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff—visited Texas to get a firsthand look at the crisis on America’s southern border.

**Unlike many in Washington** , these two leaders wanted to join President Trump in hearing directly from the people most affected, including law enforcement officers who deal with the consequences each and every day. President Trump met with Border Patrol officials in McAllen, Texas, during a trip there last month.

**“We have amazing people** down there doing a very, very difficult job,” [Acting Secretary Shanahan explained.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=80967f4397&e=3ee4985eea) U.S. officials on the border told him that barriers work and should be part of any real solution. “Any place where someone can cross the border and disappear within seconds or minutes, that's where you need barriers,” he said.

**On February 15** , President Trump declared a national emergency to address the security and humanitarian crisis at our border. Past presidents have regularly used these executive actions to confront ongoing problems: 10 such national emergencies were declared by President Obama, one of which dealt with the threat of criminal cartels—including cartels operating on our southern border.

**States have used emergency declarations** responding to crises at the border in the past, as well. In 2005, New Mexico Gov. Bill Richardson (D) declared a state of emergency along the border, claiming the area had “been devastated by the ravages and terror” of human trafficking, drug smuggling, kidnapping, murder, and other crimes.

**Now it is time** for the Federal Government to step up and do its part.

[There’s a crisis at our border—and President Trump is ensuring we end it. ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8618c1453d&e=3ee4985eea)

**Watch:** [Texas sheriff explains how illegal immigration hurts his community.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=afff9345df&e=3ee4985eea)

|

name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: This is bad (Trump's fundraising)
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
body:
He may be failing at leading the country, but he's succeeding in stockpiling money for his re-election campaign.
|
---
| | | | _"President 's re-election committee, supportive groups have already obliterated fundraising norms."_ \-- Center for Public Integrity; 2/18/2019
---
Trump may be failing as a leader, but he's certainly succeeding in raising money for his re-election, already.
We can't let Trump and his allies get too big of a head-start on their fundraising. The reality is, money matters in elections -- and we can't fall behind.
[**We're looking to raise $10,000 in February to continue to build our local and national grassroots organization. Can you rush in a contribution to help?**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113c21/622b7b5b/1ede1873/307b720f/2057829915/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse190226?refcode=dwse190226_m&recurring=true&amount=5")
_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

**[Express Donate: $25](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113c21/622b7b5b/1ede1873/307b720e/2057829915/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiI4NjE3NjkiLCJlM3R6YkhWbmZYMD0iOiJkd3NlMTkwMjI2IiwiZTN0aGJXOTFiblI5ZlE9PSI6IjI1IiwiZTN0MlpYSnphVzl1ZlgwPSI6Im0ifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dwse190226?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=dwse190226_m_25&refcode2=861769")**

Trump's trampling on our Constitution. He's trying to subvert Congress's authority with his ridiculous emergency declaration. He's attacking our First Amendment rights of free speech and free press by attacking news outlets he disagrees with.
It's chaos in this Administration -- and it's not the way our government should function.
I'm doing everything I can to protect our families and our values in this Trump era of dysfunction. But the absolute best thing we can do right now is build up an organization ready to speak out now and elect Democrats later.
If Trump wins a second term in office, it CANNOT be because we didn't have the resources to match him.
[**Rush in your contribution right now.**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113c21/622b7b5b/1ede1873/307b720f/2057829915/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse190226?refcode=dwse190226_m&recurring=true&amount=5")
Thank you.
Debbie

name: Nina Turner, Our Revolution
subject: Feeling the Bern
body:
For almost two years I have traveled across the country to states including Hawai'i, Alaska, California, Arizona, North Carolina, Iowa, South Carolina and New Hampshire because the political revolutio
# [](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a58f/bed43eb/9b4b888b/75fb152f/755571905/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ourrevolution?refcode=email-header-OR")
Friends,
For almost two years I have traveled across the country to states including Hawai'i, Alaska, California, Arizona, North Carolina, Iowa, South Carolina and New Hampshire because the political revolution begins with you -- the grassroots. As your president, I have brought your stories of success and struggle with me in our collective pursuit to build a progressive movement.
**Together we have grown Our Revolution into a powerful grassroots organization made up of people from all walks of life. Now more than ever we need you to continue the work we started.**
[**Contribute $5, $10, $27 or whatever you can afford today to help us on this mission.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a58f/bed43eb/9b4b888b/75fb152e/755571905/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-snt?refcode=em190225-full&noskip=true")
As you may have heard, I am taking a leave of absence to serve as a co-chair for Senator Sanders 2020 campaign. I am joining his team to make a tangible impact on behalf of African Americans, women, working people, and others who feel left behind politically and economically. While I will be serving our movement in a new capacity, I will remain on the board of Our Revolution.
**I could not transition without letting you all know how proud I am of the work we have done together. We are the keepers of the flame and our movement is winning.**
As we all know, the stakes are too high for any of us to sit this campaign out. As brother Frederick Douglass put it, "If there is no struggle, there is no progress." We are pushing to create a system that works for the poor and working class. We are pushing to protect Mother Earth for ourselves and future generations. We are pushing to ensure that folks in this country have high quality health care and not just access to health insurance. We are pushing to create systems of political, economic and racial equality.
The struggle for progress is real and the establishment class of politicians, corporations and greedy billionaires will do everything they can to try to stop our movement and prevent Senator Sanders from becoming President of the United States of America. Therefore, we must strengthen our resolve and rededicate ourselves to the mission ahead of us, which is to secure for ourselves and our progeny a future steeped in opportunity.
[**Our Revolution will continue to organize and fight for progressive issues and candidates, Will you chip in $5 to continue the work of Our Revolution?**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a58f/bed43eb/9b4b888b/75fb152e/755571905/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-snt?refcode=em190225-full&noskip=true")
Sen. Sanders needs every single one of us to keep fighting for Medicare for All, a clean and just energy system, and to put an end to rising income inequality. **When our movement is strong we can all rise together.** When our broad coalition works hand-in-hand there is nothing we cannot accomplish.
Thank you for all you have done and will do. I look forward to seeing you on the campaign trail.
In solidarity,
Nina Turner
President
Our Revolution

subject: Democrats Disregard Human Life
body:
**New Hampshire Democrats Disregard Human Life** **For Immediate Release:** February 26, 2019 **Contact:** Joe Sweeney 603-225-9341 [Joe@nhgop.org](mailto:joe@nhgop.org) **Concord, NH** \- NHGOP Chairman Stephen Stepanek released the following statement regarding Senators Shaheen and Hassan voting against the Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act: "By voting against and blocking the Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act, Senators Shaheen and Hassan revealed themselves to be nothing more than puppets of the extreme pro-abortion lobby. The Born-Alive Act would ensure that, if a baby is born during an abortion procedure, that human life is protected. Our U.S. Senators long avoided comment on the disgusting comments made last month by disgraced Virginia Democrat Ralph Northam; their votes last night, showing that they and the vast majority of their Democrat caucus support the murder of children born alive, shows their true colors when it comes to infanticide." "It is sickening and a sad day for the Granite State - our Senators would rather play partisan politics than protect the most vulnerable in our society." _###_

subject: President Trump Has Made Unthinkable Progress on North Korea
body:
"Since 1949, the United States and our allies have been engaged in a complicated, high-stakes efforts to reduce the global threat..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2e621936ef&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **President Trump Has Made Unthinkable Progress on North Korea**

“Since 1949, the United States and our allies have been engaged in a complicated, high-stakes efforts to reduce the global threat posed by North Korea. The result? An isolated authoritarian regime with a sophisticated nuclear arsenal many thought unachievable,” Rep. Brian Babin (R-TX) writes in _USA Today_.

“Enter President Donald Trump, who was elected to shake up the status quo. With his bold new tack in dealing with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un, we are closer to discovering a path to peace than we’ve been in decades. That progress was unthinkable little more than two years ago.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=06b7416e9a&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| Yesterday, Vice President Mike Pence traveled to Bogota, Colombia to meet with Venezuelan interim President Juan Guaido and announce new sanctions against the Maduro regime, Emily Ward reports for the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bc20e85a69&e=3ee4985eea)_. “To the people of Venezuela, including members of the Armed Forces: The United States of America stands with you if you will stand for freedom,” the Vice President said.
---
|
---
| “President Donald Trump reacted on Twitter Monday evening after the Born-Alive Survivors Protection Act, which would have ensured that babies who survive abortions are given medical attention, failed to pass the Senate,” Evie Fordham reports in _[The Daily Caller](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=dc90487748&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Senate Democrats just voted against legislation to prevent the killing of newborn infant children . . . this will be remembered as one of the most shocking votes in the history of Congress,” the President tweeted.
---
|
---
| “An alleged member of the MS-13 gang wanted in connection with murder, attempted murder and extortion was arrested in Pensacola last week,” Kevin Robinson writes in the _[Pensacola News Journal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6fc262668f&e=3ee4985eea)_. The charges are “tied to a 2018 indictment charging more than two dozen alleged MS-13 members of racketeering activity that included murders, attempted murders, conspiracies to commit murder, extortion, robbery, kidnapping, drug trafficking and money laundering.”
---
|
---
| “Proponents of the ‘Green New Deal’ have called it ambitious and visionary, comparing it to putting a man on the moon,” Daniel Turner writes in _[RealClearEnergy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d7d036a68a&e=3ee4985eea)_. “The opposite is true. From its rosy portrayal of socialist policies like a government takeover of healthcare to its calls to bring back mid-20th century labor policies, the ‘Green New Deal’ is the most backwards-looking plan the left has put forward in decades.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b84fb3e91b&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=07f39cfaf9&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=e0d85a3363)
---

**_Original email from Kamala Harris, February 23, 2019**_
With just a few days left until we close the books for February, I am reaching out with a modest request. My team is telling me we’re behind on the internal fundraising goals we set for our campaign this month.
That’s why, in a moment, I am going to ask you to make a grassroots donation to our campaign for president. But first, I hope you will let me explain why it is so important.
Our campaign is not taking a dime from corporate PACs or lobbyists -- and that was a very deliberate choice. Yes, it means we are leaving money on the table. But that’s ok with me. **I never want there to be any question about whether I’m listening to the people or corporate lobbyists. The answer will always be _the people_.**
However, this means we need to raise that money in other ways -- often with the help of people who donate to emails just like this one. Grassroots contributions aren’t just sustaining our movement: they’re powering it.
Right now, this race is wide open. The choices we make now in these early months will set the tone and pace for the rest of our campaign. And I am running to win -- so we are pushing this team as hard as we can, because there is just no other option. That is why I’m asking:
**Can I count on you to make a contribution to help reach our end-of-month fundraising goal? I wouldn't ask if your support didn't make a difference:**

body:

| | |
# We have big plans for 2020!

Over the past couple of weeks, our team has been meeting and putting together our plans for the 2020 election season. This is going to be a big election season, filled with presidential candidates (quite a few of them) and candidates up and down the ballot, which is why our team is planning on having a big presence all throughout 2019 and 2020.

We are planning on making strides in connecting with working families and elevating the issues that matter the most. Healthcare, Childcare, and Eldercare are cornerstone issues that we will be focusing on as we hold town halls, public forums, and knock on doors. We also will be focusing on bringing climate change to center stage as we highlight the job creation opportunities that come along with a real investment in renewable energy and the technology of the future.

We need your help to make our goals a reality. Which is why I am asking you to [donate today](https://IanToddforcongress.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0824156d3c3076099692cab73&id=5563464ffa&e=c56804a14d) so that we can come into the 2020 election stronger than ever, and show that CD6 has what it takes to bring bold ideas to Congress!

Thank you so much for your support.

Your friend,

_Ian A. Todd_

The team emailed yesterday asking for you to [ https://act.betoorourke.com/survey/survey/?t=1001&akid=24953%2E444211%2E9uZHbR ]fill out our one-minute
survey. Now I’m following up because I want to make sure everyone has a
chance to share their thoughts before Amy and I take a look at the
results. Thousands of you have already taken the time to let us know what
issues are most important to you and how you plan to get involved in the
next election.

Thanks to those of you who’ve done so already.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/survey/survey/?t=1002&akid=24953%2E444211%2E9uZHbR ]Take the survey »

I’ve found that the best way to learn is to listen to everyone. To have
open discussions regardless of any of the differences that might otherwise
divide us. To open ourselves up to different perspectives. And to ask
questions so I can understand what’s on your mind, where you want our
country to go, the ideas you have for getting it there.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/survey/survey/?t=1003&akid=24953%2E444211%2E9uZHbR ]So please take a second to fill out the survey before the end of the

name: Sherrod Brown
subject: republicans' attack on reproductive rights
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
body:
|
---

| | Peter -- A woman's reproductive health decisions should be between her and her doctor. But Republicans continue to insert themselves in those discussions -- and the bill they tried to pass yesterday is just the latest example. Last night, Republicans tried to pass legislation that would have targeted and intimidated reproductive healthcare providers, putting women's health at risk. [**It failed -- because people like you raised your voices against harmful legislation like this. But this will undoubtedly not be the last time Republicans attempt to make it more difficult for women to access the care they need. Say you 'll keep fighting back against their efforts.**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffc30/4462dc26/8dc5dc03/201ed95c/4234596807/VEsH/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e190226b") We should trust women to make their own healthcare choices. But time and again, Republicans push for the opposite. They've repeatedly attempted to defund Planned Parenthood. They supported the repeal of the Affordable Care Act, a move that would have made it much harder for millions of women to get the care they need. Yesterday, they tried to restrict women's access to comprehensive care and make their own judgments part of a discussion between a family and their doctor. It's unconscionable. [**I have always -- and will always -- defend a woman 's right to make her own decisions about her health, and I'm ready to fight back against any efforts that restrict that freedom. If you're with me, say so by signing our petition.**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffc30/4462dc26/8dc5dc03/201ed95c/4234596807/VEsE/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e190226b") With gratitude, Sherrod
